pages.raw_text:
At last nights meeting of the
trades council no report was received
from the plumbers on the trouble
with the gas company. The black
smiths union was recognized and
three delegates were seated represent
ing that body.


A report was received from the In
ternational body of the walters union
endorsing the new scale and the
agreement of the, local body.


President Carr was present to an
swer the charges preferred against
him by the gas workers union. Il. l..
Quinn stated there was on hand suf-
ficient evidence to substantiate the
truth of each allegation made against
Mr.. Carr, There was considerable
discussion precipitated. but as most
of the witnesses who promised to be
in attendance did not appear. the case
was postponed until the next meet
ing


Speaking to Press reporter this
morning. Mr., Carr said:


Every charge against me Is UNI
